**1. Analyze the image:**
*   **Header:** "Basel, den 12. März 1928." -> This is the date and location.
*   **Recipient:** "An die Basler Rheinschiffahrt A.G., Basel." -> This is the receiver.
*   **Body:** "Wir bekennen uns zum Empfang Ihres Schreibens vom 1.ds. und teilen Ihnen höflich mit, dass dessen Behandlung auf der Traktandenliste der Vorstands-Sitzung vom 13. März steht." (We acknowledge receipt of your letter from the 1st of this month and inform you politely that its treatment is on the agenda of the board meeting on March 13th.)
*   **Closing:** "Wir werden nicht verfehlen Ihnen in Kürze weitere Mitteilungen zukommen zu lassen und zeichnen hochachtungsvoll:" (We will not fail to send you further communications shortly and sign with high respect:)
*   **Signature/Stamp:** There is a faint stamp at the bottom right. It reads "Basler Personenschiffahrtsgesellschaft" and below that "Der Geschäftsführer". This indicates the sender is the "Basler Personenschiffahrtsgesellschaft" (Basel Passenger Shipping Company), specifically the Managing Director ("Der Geschäftsführer"). Since no specific person's name is signed, the entity is the sender.

**2. Extract the required fields:**

*   **letter_title:** There is no explicit title like "Subject: ...". It's a standard business letter acknowledging receipt. I will set this to null as there is no specific title provided in the text.
*   **sender_persons:** The stamp says "Basler Personenschiffahrtsgesellschaft" and "Der Geschäftsführer". So the sender is the company or the managing director of that company. I will use the company name as the sender entity.
*   **send_date:** "12. März 1928". In ISO format: 1928-03-12.
*   **receiver_persons:** "An die Basler Rheinschiffahrt A.G.". This is the receiver.

*   `send_date`: ["1928-03-12"]
*   `receiver_persons`: ["Basler Rheinschiffahrt A.G."]
